issues.title: dichotomy between VPNs and overlays in Appendix A.
issues.author: smishra1200
issues.body:
Logging an issue per David Schinazi <dschinazi.ietf@gmail.com> Mon, 23 February 2026 22:44 UTC

I disagree with the dichotomy between VPNs and overlays in
Appendix A. VPNs are just one category of network overlay. As an example,
if Chrome were to ship a default-enabled full-tunnel VPN to Google
datacenters for all users tomorrow, I can guarantee that it'll cause the
issues mentioned in other parts of the doc. Also, an IPsec VPN will reduce
the MTU, that's something that can adversely impact video performance.
There's still recent work in L3/L2 VPNs, such as RFC 9484 and
draft-ietf-masque-connect-ethernet is a new layer 2 VPN. The fact that they
both use the QUIC packet format instead of ESP or L2TP header doesn't
change the performance characteristics. The activation of  the QUIC
congestion controller could do interesting things, but so can a traffic
shaper that only drops packets. At the end of the day, I think the
distinction is semantics and doesn't matter when it comes to what
recommendations you're giving.

pulls.title: First round of mostly editorial changes from Jay Robertson
pulls.author: jkrsvta

pulls.title: Fix incorrect RFC reference (RFC7264 to RFC7624) in Introduction
pulls.author: smishra1200
pulls.body:
Corrects an incorrect reference in the Introduction flagged by David Schinazi in his review of -03.

The Introduction cited RFC7264 in two places where it should be RFC7624 ("Confidentiality in the Face of Pervasive Surveillance"), which is the RFC that explored the threats from RFC7258 and outlined mitigations. Section 2 already uses RFC7624 correctly, so this brings the Introduction in line.

No other changes (grammar and editorial items from the review will follow separately).

pulls.title: Editorial/nits updates in the Introduction
pulls.author: smishra1200
pulls.body: Editorial and nits updates to the Introduction. No technical or normative changes.

pulls.title: Clarify document purpose as a problem statement (per David Schinazi's review)
pulls.author: smishra1200
pulls.body:
Addresses David Schinazi's review of -03 on the MOPS list (2026-02-23), specifically his question about the document's call to action and target audience:

> I'm not entirely sure what the call for action is. Is the target audience video streaming operators? ... If the target audience is designers of the privacy tech, what action are they supposed to take? Right now, I feel like you identify real issues but then just leave the reader thinking "oh well, that's a shame".

Per Glenn's framing that this document is a problem statement intended to raise awareness, this rewrites the closing Introduction paragraph to state the purpose plainly (raise awareness; problem statement), name the audiences including designers of privacy-enhancing technologies, and scope solution and mitigation design as out of scope and future work.

This PR addresses only the call-to-action and purpose point. Other items from David's review are not part of this PR and are being considered separately.

pulls.title: Fix grammar in Introduction (Protocols such as QUIC ... are examples)
pulls.author: smishra1200
pulls.body: Editorial grammar fix in the first Introduction paragraph: 'Protocol such as QUIC {{!RFC9000}} is an examples' becomes 'Protocols such as QUIC {{!RFC9000}} are examples'. No other changes.

pulls.title: Add Transparency and Connection Policy section, remove Appendix A (is…
pulls.author: smishra1200
pulls.body:
Closes #6

  Replaces Appendix A (Network Overlays are different than VPNs) with a
  new Section 2.2 (Transparency and Connection Policy). The new section
  reframes the VPN vs overlay distinction around transparency of
  connection policy changes to endpoints, addresses David Schinazi's
  review comment on the VPN dichotomy.

reviews.author: gitnnelg
reviews.body: This is an improvement from the original text and addresses the issue David S. raised.

pulls.title: Editorial: clarify opening sentence of connection autonomy paragraph of Transparency and Connection Policy section
pulls.author: smishra1200
pulls.body:
Transparency and Connection Policy (5th paragraph)
Replace:
"A related distinction concerns end-to-end connection autonomy" with
"Network overlays also affect end-to-end connection autonomy"

Use of "related distinction" is very abstract and may confuse the reader. The new sentence keeps the context unambiguous.
